observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Record all procured tangible items costing ₱50,000.00 under the Semi-Expendable property account, and subsequently derecognize the asset account with the recognition of the expense account upon the issuance thereof to the end-users;
Reclassify all tangible items costing ₱50,000.00 acquired prior to CY 2024 under Semi-Expendable Property account, and later derecognized under Prior Period Adjustment account for those items issued to the end-user with Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS); and
Maintain a Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card for those semi-expendable properties.
We also recommended that the Property Custodian:
Issue an Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to the end-users of the semi-expendable property to establish the accountability thereof;
Forward the Municipal Accountant with the copies of issued ICS for reclassification purposes of semi-expendable properties in the books of accounts; and
Maintain a Semi-Expendable Property Card for those semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Disposal Committee:
Submit the necessary documents pertaining to the proper disposal procedures of the demolished Old Public Market for evaluation;
Furnish the above documents to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the derecognition of the asset from the books of accounts; and
If the required documents cannot be submitted for audit, provide a written justification explaining the reasons for their unavailability.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant derecognize the demolished Old Public Market in the books of accounts upon receipt of the related documents from the Disposal Committee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take immediate steps to secure and formalize its ownership over the newly constructed Municipal Public Market by executing and registering the necessary legal documents, such as a Deed of Donation, Deed of Transfer, or any other applicable instrument, to reflect the proper ownership in its name, to avoid any impediments or encumbrances on the property.
Once the ownership of the property is settled, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the Municipality’s books of account of the subject asset, to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the financial statements, in accordance with IPSAS and applicable government accounting policies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned DBM-DOF-DILG JMC, to ensure the optimal achievement of the Municipality’s socio-economic development and environmental management goals while effectively utilizing the 20% Development Fund for its intended purposes.

For some deficiencies mentioned above, we recommended that the Members of the MDRRM Council strictly adherence with Republic Act No. 10121 and other applicable rules and regulations to strengthen the capabilities of the local government units and its constituents for disaster preparedness, mitigations, response and recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Department Heads ensure that insurable properties of the LGU are covered under the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) to guarantee that any loss or damage will be properly indemnified by the insurer.

We recommended that the Members of the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Budget Officer, ensure that all GAD programs, projects, and activities listed in the GAD Plan and Budget are fully integrated into the Annual Investment Plan and the Annual Budget.
We also recommended that the Members of the GAD Focal Point System ensure the optimal implementation of all GAD programs, projects, and activities to effectively address gender issues and fulfill the GAD mandate within the community.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order for the establishment of an Internal Policies, Guidelines, and Limitations on the Travel Expenses for JO Personnel.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer:
Stipulate in the contract the travel rates and terms to be agreed upon by the JO personnel and the LGU for the bases of the total allowable amount of travelling expenses to be claimed by the JO personnel; and
Approve the Travel Order of the JO Personnel whose contracts were explicitly authorize to undertake an official travel
Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant review thoroughly the travel claims of the JO Personnel to ensure that the travels are properly approved by the authorized official and the claims are duly supported with the documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Management Officer and Electronic Remittance File Handler:
conduct a thorough verification, analysis and validation between the GSIS billings, withholdings/contributions and remittances of the individual employees;
remit those unremitted withholdings and contributions once the GSIS WebMSP data was updated, and reimburse/refund from the concerned employees on the noted discrepancies; and
coordinate with concerned employees for the noted discrepancies for the reimbursement or collections thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Treasurer:
conduct a regular reconciliation between the PhilHealth billings, contributions and remittances of the individual employees;
reimburse/refund from the concerned employees on the noted discrepancies, if applicable, as well as, the immediate remittance of the unremitted PhilHealth premium contributions; and
coordinate with the concerned employees on the accounts that has deficiencies with the PhilHealth records, including the settlement of the previous arrearages, if appropriate.

We recommended that the Local School Board develop a three-year program outlining strategic prioritization policies for allocating the SEF among schools within the municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination of the Municipal Budget Officer, submit the following certified statement to the LSB not later than July 15 of the current year, to wit
Actual SEF Income for the immediately preceding fiscal year;
Actual SEF Income for the first two quarters of the current year;
Estimated SEF Income for the last two quarters of the current year; and
Annual Estimates for the ensuing year
We further recommended that the LSB review and approve the SEF Budget for the ensuing year not later than October 31 of the current year to ensure timely and effective planning and budgeting of the SEF.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, ensure that the subsistence allowance is accurately computed and granted only for the actual days of service rendered.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and other Social Workers regularly submit the required supporting documents on the claims of Hazard Pay.
We also recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer ensure that Hazard Pay is properly adjusted to exclude days when employees were on leave of absence or official travel. Additionally, all payments should be supported with the necessary documentation to validate actual exposure to hazardous conditions, in compliance with applicable regulations.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough review on the payments of Hazard Pay and ensure the correct amount of claims with complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 1.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts, and record the adjustments to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data in the financial statements.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned continuously adhere to the provisions of the aforementioned DBM-DOF-DILG JMC, to ensure that maximum attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality are achieved and the intended purposes of the 20% Development Fund are realized.

3.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer and BAC Secretariat prepare and attach to the Bidding Documents and DVs, the following lacking documents for all the procurements in CY 2023:
a. Certification by the head of the BAC Secretariat that the Invitation to Bid was posted at any conspicuous place reserved for the purpose in the premises of the LGU for 7 calendar days
b. Performance Security (should be attached to the Contract Agreement)
c. Construction Methods
d. Construction safety and health program approved by the Department of Labor and Employment
e. Certified copy of the page of the approved Annual Procurement Plan,
f. Warranty Security,
g. Certificate of Acceptance by the Agency, and
h. Statement of Time Elapsed.

3.9. Henceforth, we recommended that the concerned officials ensure that the above-listed documents are prepared for all future procurements.

3.10. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant only process Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documentations.

5.19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer:
a. submit a justification for the noted delays; and
b. impose and collect the corresponding amount of liquidated damages from the supplier or contractor, if warranted.

5.20. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the compliance of the Contractor on the terms of the contract, including the imposition of liquidated damages for the violation thereof, if warranted.

5.21. We finally recommended that the Municipal Mayor include only those proposed development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan.

6.11. We recommended that the BAC submit the following documents on the procurement of goods and services under the Negotiated Procurement-Small Value Procurement, to wit:
a. Proof that the Request for Quotations (RFQs) were sent to at least three (3) suppliers;
b. Proof that at least one (1) quotations were received from the suppliers who responded the RFQ; and
c. Abstract of Quotation that showed the names of those who responded the RFQ and the corresponding price quotations.

6.12. We also recommended that the BAC post the RFQ with Approve Budget for the Contract (ABC) above Fifty Thousand Pesos (₱50,000.00) for a period of three (3) calendar days in the PhilGEPS website, website of the Procuring Entity, if available, and at any conspicuous place in the premises of the municipality.

6.13. We further recommended that the BAC prepare a separate the BAC Resolution justifying and recommending the change of the mode of procurement and the BAC resolution recommending to the LCE on the award of the contract in favor of the supplier or contractor with the Single or Lowest Calculated and Responsive Quotation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the mandatory research and planning and operations and warning positions, which may be augmented through any or a combination stated in the Section 5.5 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 to provide adequate support to DRRM activities.

9.10. We recommended that the LDRRM Officer closely monitor the disbursement of the LDDRM Fund, in coordination with the Accounting Department, and immediately prepare the Report of Receipts and Utilizations of the fund, to determine the amount of unexpended balance as at year-end.

9.11. We also recommended that the LDRMM Officer initiate the immediate transfer of the unexpended balance of the fund in CY 2023 in the amount ₱1,788,005.25 to the MDRRM Trust Fund Account.

10.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all Officials concerned initiate the procurement of the raw materials, equipment and tools, in order to start the implementation of the livelihood development project, otherwise, return the unused funds to the DOLE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare necessary adjustment for the overstated balance of the Due to NGA account by ₱9,162,130.00 in the books of account to correct the presentation thereof in the financial statements; and
regularly check the journal entries made in the accounting system to avoid any inaccurate recognition in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare the adjusting entries to revert the amount of unreconciled items previously recognized in the books of accounts that were recorded without supporting documents or detailed information thereof;
prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement for CY 2015 and previous years in order to identify the specific reconciling items in the SEF and TF books; and
henceforth, prepare the necessary adjusting entries only when all the reconciling items have been identified.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
prepare the annual Schedule of the delinquent lot lessees and market stallholders as at yearend and furnish the same to the Municipal Accountant; and
prepare Demand Letters indicating the total unpaid monthly rentals to all delinquent stallholders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts the receivables on the rentals from the Government Lots and the Market Stall, that are already due for collections, based on the report forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer and the corresponding income earned therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor:
prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer, on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, the assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties; and
immediately make the necessary corrections on the noted errors in the recording of real properties in the iTax System.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
update the Taxpayer’s Index Card and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Assessors;
submit the Taxpayer’s Index Card to the Municipal Accountant at the start of each year as the basis in recognition of the RPT Receivables; and
update the data on Integrated Taxation (iTax) Management System.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record every January of each year the amount of RPT and SET due and collectible as receivables, based on the computed amount per RPT Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index cards.

We recommended that the BAC prepare a BAC Resolution recommending the HOPE for the award of contract in favor of the supplier or contractor with the Single or Lowest Calculated and Responsive Quotation and observe the proper awarding procedures provided in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and the LMDRRM Council submit written justifications on why the LDRRMF Investment Program was only prepared in December 16, 2022.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer, through the LDRRM Council, prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan under separate caption for the PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, so that the unexpended balances available for use for disaster-related projects.
We also recommended that the LDRRM Council review the balances of the Continuing Appropriations in the preparation of the annual budget, so that any appropriations for projects which should not be charged against the LDRRMF or which could no longer be implemented can be re-appropriated for other priority projects which are disaster related.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and submit a copy of the same to the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant
We also recommended that the Property Custodian, assist the Municipal Health Officer in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any.
We further recommend that the Property Custodian maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents and prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor thru the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Health Officer to submit the Daily Record of Attendance of all the beneficiaries of the One Covid-19 Allowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Immediately remit the Barangays’ share from the RPT collections in CY 2022; and
henceforth, release the Barangays’ shares from the RPT collections within five days after the end of each quarter and shall not be subject to any lien or holdback for whatever purpose.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers of the Due to LGU account for the detail breakdown of the shares for the Province and each Barangay.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, identify the detailed composition on the balances of the Due to LGUs account as of December 31, 2022, especially those balances from prior years, and immediately remit the share for the Province and the Barangays.

We recommended that the Collecting Officers:
submit a written explanation on the delayed remittance of collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and
henceforth, remit all collections at the close of each day to the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from possible loss through misuse or misappropriation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
diligently review and verify the RCDs submitted by the Collecting Officers and meticulously validate the accountable forms for any unremitted collections;
for control purposes, refrain the issuance/release of another unused accountable forms to the Collecting Officers, unless collections issued with previous accountable forms are fully remitted; and
prepare daily the consolidated RCDs and transmit the same and its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant, every 5th day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer requires the Collecting Officers to issue the accountable forms strictly in chronological order based on the actual date of collections, including the duly acknowledgement of accountable forms in the logbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
thru the authority from the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, execute a Contract of Lease with the Lessees of government Lots and Stallholders of the Public Market;
thru the members of the Market Committee, establish an effective and efficient measures to increase and encourage more vendors in the Public Market;
thru the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan and members of the Market Committee, update the Revenue Code, specially on the Municipal charges for government lots and Public Market; and
thru the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Treasurer, install a visible identification number for each sections of the Public Market.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
immediately collects the rentals from all lessees and stallholders, including, the imposition of the penalties, if warranted
strictly monitor the payments of the monthly rental fees of each lessees and stallholders; and
issue Demand Letters for the delinquent lessees and stallholders.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the accountable officers, especially those with long overdue cash advances, and those outgoing elected officials and employees before issuing their certificates of clearance, to immediately settle or refund their unliquidated cash advance, otherwise, cause the withholding of the salary pursuant to 2nd Paragraph of Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances; and
Initiate the request for write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances, thru the Municipal Accountant, especially to those accountable officers who have already died and/or absconded, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, but only after exhausting all possible remedies.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare individual subsidiary ledgers for each Accountable Officer with proper disclosure or classification whether they are still employed, have resigned or deceased, in order for the Municipality to properly plan the courses of action to be made in the settlement of the cash advances.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal HRMO undertake the following measures:
Ensure that if there was an employment of Contract of Service, it should be aligned with Republic Act No. 9184;
Ensure that employment of Job Order workers uses the pro-forma agreement as required under Section 9 of the CSC Resolution No. 021430 dated November 12, 2002; and
Indicate the complete name of the COS or JO workers, which includes the middle or initial name and any suffix name after the last name, both in the agreement and the payroll to avoid any confusion or duplication.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents for the payment of COS or JO workers.

1.27.2. Compute depreciation of each specific property in the PPEs and furnish
the Audit Team with a copy thereof every January of each year;
1.27.3. Maintain a GL for Accumulated Depreciation of each specific property
PPE account to provide proof of the reflected amounts;
1.27.4. Present the Book Value of each PPE accounts and the related
Accumulated Depreciation in the Notes to the FS instead of lumpsum.
1.28. We also reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Designated
Property Officer:
1.28.1. issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt for every equipment issued
to an employee to track the custodians of LGU assets and renew all PAR
every after three years to account for any losses.
1.28.2. mark assets with Property Tags counted during Physical Inventory.
1.29. The management commented that it takes time to trace back all the procurement of
PPEs of the LGU but the Municipal Accountant will provide subsidiary ledgers as
per recommendation. Also, they agreed to act on all the cited recommendations.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the list of creditors
to support the balance of Accounts Payable, including the year when the
liabilities was incurred, otherwise, revert the amount which are not supported
with valid claimants. Also, record transactions in the GL correctly and
completely and update the totals and balances monthly.

3.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and validate past
records in order to account the details of the negative balances in the Due to
GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts to avoid the possibility that the LGU
funds were used for such remittances. If determined to be mere errors in
posting, prepare the necessary correcting entries to resolve the deficiency.

4.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all
department heads mandating the following:
a.) Maintain a logbook to record all supplies requisitioned, consumed and
utilized and with month-end balances of remaining stocks on hand;
b.) Submit a report of stocks on hand every June 30 and December 31 each
year to the designated Property Officer for consolidation; and
c.) Submit to the Municipal Accountant the Distribution Lists or proof of
actual receipt by the intended recipients of the goods procured for a
particular program.
4.14. We also recommended that the designated Property Officer:
a.) Prepare a pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for every
supplies issued to be signed by the Head of each requisitioning office and
summarize all RIS per month by preparing the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) monthly;
b.) Submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the following month
the SSMI, supported with the RIS, as basis in recording the issuances as
deductions in the Inventory account;
c.) Prepare a Report on Physical Count of Inventories every June 30 and
December 31 each year based on the report of stocks on hand from each
office and submit to the Municipal Accountant and to COA not later than
July 31 and January 31 of each year; and
d.) Maintain an updated and complete Stock Cards and reconcile with
accounting records monthly.
4.15. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) Record supplies and materials under the appropriate Inventory account
upon purchase and provide a sub-account for goods issued by the Property
Officer to Heads of Offices for implementation of programs;
b.) Record the equivalent expense only when supported with SSMI or
Distribution Lists from the Heads of Offices who are in charge with
particular Programs;
c.) Maintain an updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and
reconcile the same with the Stock Cards of the Property Officer; and
d.) Reconcile balances of Inventory accounts with the RPCI

5.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the end of each
month to avoid penalties and surcharges.

6.9. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer continue their speedy
implementation of projects to expedite the realization of their intended
purposes.
6.10. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council, through the Municipal Planning Development
Coordinator, include in the Annual Investment Plan, projects which are
attainable and can be implemented during the year with sufficient estimates of
cost for each project.

7.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the LDRRM
Council, through the MDRRM Officer, include in the LDRRM Investment
Plan, appropriate and utilize the LDRRMF only the PPAs specifically
prescribed under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-1. Henceforth, purchase of office supplies and equipment should be
charged against the General Fund of the LGU.

8.11. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer:
a.) Conduct inspection of all properties of LGU-Almeria being rented to
determine the actual occupants of each stall and municipal lots;
b.) Submit to the Municipal Mayor the list of occupant as basis in the
preparation of Lease Contracts; and
c.) Collect rentals monthly and issue demand letters signed by the Municipal
Mayor to delinquent tenants, after the lapse of one month.
8.12. We further recommended the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Renew all Lease Contracts with building stall holders and lot tenants and
incorporate therein, among others, the current rates, due dates of
payments, contract period and penalties to tenants who fails to pay on time
and closure/eviction when LGU’s Demand Letter is not heeded; and
b.) Provide assistance to the Municipal Treasurer in the enforcement of
payments of rentals from delinquent tenants/stallholders.

9.7. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
a.) Prepare the necessary documents to open a separate bank account for the
LGU-Almeria water system operations, subject to approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan;
b.) Deposit all collections from water concessionaires to the said Water System
bank account.
9.8. We also recommended that the office in charge with the Municipal
Waterworks operation submit monthly the Summary of Billing Statements to
the Municipal Accountant for recording of the monthly receivables from all
water concessionaires.
9.9. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate
books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations and record
therein the monthly billings, collections and the related disbursements.
9.10. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct an inspection
of the facilities and structures being used for the water system operations and
assess its current values and submit a report thereon to the Property Officer
and the Municipal Accountant to harmonize their respective records.

10.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) Pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for
Region VIII, otherwise, reduce the number of JOs to those considered
essential and urgent only; and,
b.) Revise the Contract of Service of Job Order personnel to include
information on source of fund, daily wage, contract period, required tasks
and office assignment.
10.13. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments of
wages only when the payrolls are supported with an Accomplishment Report
from each JOs, duly signed and confirmed by the respective Head of Office to
where they are assigned; and record transactions in the proper expense
account.
10.14. We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer charged the
wages of JOs to the proper expense account that are aligned with the nature
of their services, and based on the budget indicated in their respective
Contracts of Service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:


     a. the Inventory Committee to confirm through  inspection  the  listed
        properties with the assistance of the Municipal  Assessor  and  the
        Municipal Engineer and  from  each  Division.   Also,  prepare  the
        RPCPPE using the prescribed format duly approved  by  the  LCE  and
        submit the said report within the prescribed period to the  offices
        concerned;


     b. the General Services Officer -Designate to maintain Property  Cards
        for each kind of PPE owned by  the  Agency  starting  from  current
        purchases and reconcile the same with accounting records; and

     c. the Municipal Accountant to  maintain  and  update  the  prescribed
        PPELC, starting from current purchases and reconcile the same  with
        property records.

      The Local Chief Executive committed  to  strictly  observe  the  audit
recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  provide  the  necessary
assistance to the Municipal Treasurer, in terms of  manpower,  in  order  to
intensify and maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Taxes.


       We also  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the
Municipal Treasurer to:
     1. design a strategy to enhance extensive and effective Tax Collection
        Campaign program;


     2. send notices of delinquency to each concerned property owner;


     3. post the names of delinquent property owners at the  main  entrance
        of the Municipal Hall and in such  other  publicly  accessible  and
        conspicuous places in each Barangay of the LGU;


     4. prepare a certified list of all  real  property  tax  delinquencies
        which remained uncollected or unpaid for at  least  one  (1)  year,
        stating the reason or  reasons  for  such  non-collection  or  non-
        payment; and


     5. submit the list to the Sanggunian Bayan on or before December 31 of
        the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies
        were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement  of
        the remedies for collection either by  administrative  action  thru
        levy on real property or by judicial action.

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Human  Resource
Officer to ensure that all deductions in the employees’ salaries  are  based
on the GSIS billing and to provide a recapitulation  of  all  deductions  in
the payroll for easy reference.


      Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant  scrutinize  and
review documents pertaining to  remittances  and  ensure  that  the  amounts
remitted to the GSIS are the same as the amounts withheld so that LGU  funds
are not used to  pay  for  private  loans  and  avoid  audit  disallowances.
Maintain Subsidiary Ledgers relevant  to  remittances  to  avoid  or  detect
errors.  Also, trace and reconcile prior years  transactions  affecting  the
Due To BIR account to determine the  employees  whose  remittances  were  in
excess of their deductions.

We recommended that the Municipal  Development  Council,  through  the
Municipal  Mayor  as  Chairman,  include  in  the  Annual  Investment  Plan,
projects  which  are  compliant  with  the  provisions  of  DILG-DBM   Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement all projects
embodied in the AIP during the year and for  the  Municipal  Budget  Officer
and Municipal Accountant to appropriate and  process  transactions  for  the
utilization of the 20% Development Fund only when it is  for  socio-economic
and environmental development projects.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer ensure  that  the  PPAs  in  the
LDRRMFIP and its  appropriations  as  approved  by  the  LDRRM  Council  are
adopted by the Municipal Budget Officer in the  preparation  of  the  Annual
Budget of the LGU.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer coordinate  with
the MPDC & LDRRMO in the formulation of LDRRM Budget based on  the  LDRRMFIP
approved by the Council.  Also,  ensure  that  only  PPAs  in  the  approved
LDRRMFIP are implemented and paid.


      Further, we recommended that all expenses relative to  the  operations
of the LDRRM Office be paid out  of  the  General  Fund  and  not  from  the
LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning  Development  Council,  the
Budget Officer and the SWM Focal Person ensure that the  Plans  and  Targets
relative to Solid Waste Management are congruent with the  needs  of  the
Municipality, realizable within the year and  geared  towards  an  efficient
utilization of the budget for the said program.


      Also, we recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  oversee  the
preparation of the aforementioned Plan so that the targets embodied  therein
are those which are actually  and  presently  needed  by  the  LGU  and  its
constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through  the  Municipal
Treasurer, enforce the collections of lease rentals from delinquent  tenants
of wet and dry market stalls and from all  water  consumers  by  undertaking
the following measures:
     a) Require all stallholders or tenants to renew its Lease Agreement;
     b)  Issue  Demand  Letters  to  delinquent  stallholders   and   water
        consumers, and;
     c) Strictly impose penalties and apply  the  civil  remedies  provided
        under Section 183 of R.A. 7160 and Item (b) of Section 5E.03 of the
        Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-001 which emphasizes on  disconnection
        of water supply services due to non-payment of water bills.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the  GAD  Focal
Point System of the LGU:

      a) Ensure that the GAD plan and budget is duly reviewed, validated and
         endorsed by the PCW prior to its implementation;

      b) Prepare and submit the agency’s CY 2017 GAD  Accomplishment  Report
         to the concerned offices;

      c) Plan adequately its GAD Activities  to  ensure  that  disbursements
         expended  were  the  most  beneficial   to   management   and   its
         constituents;

      d) Tap local venues and suppliers in the conduct of GAD  seminars  and
         similar activities so as to observe  austere  measures  and  frugal
         spending of government funds; and

      e) Ensure that all GAD related  activities  are  duly  supported  with
         complete and necessary documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  strictly  require  the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit  immediately  the
lacking disbursement vouchers and its supporting  documents.   Also,  comply
diligently with the timeline set for the submission of  trial  balances  and
other financial reports provided for in Sections 122 and  127  of  PD  1445,
Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular 2009-006,  and  pertinent  provisions  of  COA
Circular No. 2010-001.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  all  employees  to
exercise prudence when  filing  claims  for  official  travels  and  observe
strictly the existing provisions of Executive Order 248 and 298.


      We also recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  review  thoroughly
the completeness of  the  supporting  documents  and  ensure  the  legality,
correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before  payments
for traveling expense are approved.

      Henceforth, require the submission of ORs as basis to  support  claims
for taxi fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  stop  the  practice  of
granting incentives to the RCCs,  or  other  employees  similarly  situated,
without legal basis, in consonance with the provisions of Section 12  of RA
No.  6758  to  avoid  irregular  expenses  which  are   subject   to   audit
disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all the  members  of
the Local School  Board  ensure  that  programs  and  activities  for  Early
Childhood Care Development are incorporated in the budget  for  SEF.   Also,
discontinue the use of SEF for  expenses  which  are  not  authorized  under
DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute the  Local  Council
for the Protection of Children in the LGU from among its  department  heads,
with himself as the Chairman, and require its members to  identify  programs
and projects for the protection and  welfare  of  children  and  prevention  of
juvenile delinquency through the preparation of the  LCPC  Annual  Work  and
Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in  the  local
development plan.

      Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the
programs/projects  contained  in  the  AWFP  of   the   LCPC   for   the
implementation of its programs is equivalent to 1% of  the  IRA  of  the
Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.
observations.reason: Some projects are still for procuremen t stage and 2 failed biddings.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to submit an explanation for the non-implementati on of development projects. Also, the Municipal Mayor ensure full implementation of all priority projects in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the fund and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcome for its constituents. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the Budget Officer.

We recommend that the LDRRMO ensure the implementation of all activities in the LDRRMFIP. Also, submit the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local School Board should give equal importance to all priority expenditures enumerated by law in the utilization of SEF by ensuring that such would be allocated correspondingly in the preparation of the Annual School Board Budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial Government of Biliran the required quarterly accomplishment report and the Municipal RYF Council shall prepare the updated 6-year updated strategic plan in order that the objective/vision , mission and major final output of this program was fully  attained.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor issue a Memorandum to all accountable officers requiring them the full liquidation of their respective cash advances. Specifically, require the Municipal Accountant to carry out the following courses of actions: For purposes of monitoring, retain a copy of the cash advance voucher and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the cash advances made by each official/employee. Send, within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30-day period to liquidate, a written reminder signed by the Municipal Mayor, requiring the official/employee concerned to liquidate his cash advance. Issue Demand Letters to those who refuse to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. Continued disregard of the demand letters should cause the withholding of any money due the concerned personnel.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, being the LCE, should act with urgency in the mandatory closure of the existing open dumpsite as he may be held liable for violating the solid waste management law.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should regularly appoint a DRRM Officer as required by law.
observations.reason: Waiting with the qualified applicant as required by Law.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat submit the required documents as mandated by COA Regulations. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the immediate submission of the said documents.
We recommend that Management should make a responsive and concrete written policy in granting monetization benefits in such a manner that all of its employees will be benefited.
We recommend that the designated AWS Manager refund the honorarium that was paid to him.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat submit the required documents as mandated by COA Regulations.  We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the immediate submission of the said documents.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace, verify   and
investigate the  discrepancies  between  the  balances  per  books  and  per
inventory report  and  effect  appropriate  adjustments  to  reconcile  both
records.  We also  reiterate  our  prior  year’s  recommendations  requiring
Management to conduct the physical inventory of PPE at  least  once  a  year
and submit a report on the results thereon.  We further recommend  that  the
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) on issued properties be  renewed
every three (3) years.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  each  program  and
project  coordinator  to  fast-track  the  implementation  of  programs  and
projects funded by the National Government in accordance with  the  timeline
provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the  Liquidation  Reports
and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal  Engineer
to  submit  an  explanation  for  the  non-implementation   of   development
projects.  Also, the Municipal  Mayor  ensure  full  implementation  of  all
priority projects in the AIP in order to maximize  the  utilization  of  the
fund and achieve  desirable  socio-economic  development  and  environmental
outcome for its constituents.

      We also recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  maintain  Subsidiary
Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for  all  disbursements  and
balances of the  fund  and  reconcile  immediately  the  balances  from  the
records of the Budget Officer.

We  recommend  that  the  LDRRMO  ensure  the   implementation   ofall
activities in the LDRRMFIP.  Also, submit the  Report  on  the  Receipt  and
Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  Funds  on  or  before
the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC  and  the  Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.


      The LDRRMO assured the Audit Team that the programs  and  projects  in
the LDRRMIP will be fully implemented in order to utilize fully  the  LDRRMF
in accordance with the purpose.

We recommend that the Local School Board should give equal  importance
to all priority expenditures enumerated by law in the utilization of SEF  by
ensuring that such would be allocated correspondingly in the preparation  of
the Annual School Board Budget.

We  recommend  that   the  Municipal  Accountant  request  from  the
Provincial Government  of  Biliran  the  required  quarterly  accomplishment
report and the Municipal  RYF  Council  shall  prepare  the  updated  6-year
updated strategic plan in order  that   the  objective/vision,  mission  and
major final output of this program was fully  attained.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  issue  a  Memorandum  to  all
accountable  officers  requiring  them  the  full   liquidation   of   their
respective cash advances.  Specifically, require  the  Municipal  Accountant
to carry out the following courses of actions:

   a) For purposes of monitoring, retain a copy of the cash advance  voucher
      and itinerary of travel, as well as keep an index of the cash advances
      made by each official/employee.


   b) Send, within ten (10) days before the expiration of the 30-day  period
      to liquidate, a  written  reminder  signed  by  the  Municipal  Mayor,
      requiring  the  official/employee  concerned  to  liquidate  his  cash
      advance.


   c) Issue Demand Letters to those  who  refuse  to  liquidate  their  cash
      advances within the prescribed  period.  Continued  disregard  of  the
      demand letters should cause the  withholding  of  any  money  due  the
      concerned personnel.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, to refrain from signing the Contract or Memorandum of Agreement with the winning bidder before its issuance of the related Performance Security.  We also recommend the submission of the original copies of the Performance bonds to further verify its entries.  This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Disallowance whenever necessary.
We recommend that the APP-CSE should be submitted regularly every November 30 of each year so as to ensure the availability of the projected CSE in the DBM-PS Sub Depot.

We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, ensures that all documents required pertaining to procurement be completely attached in the claims made by Contractors.  Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should be more circumspect in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the AWS Manager refrain from proposing a budget for AWS until an Ordinance is enacted for its creation.  We also recommend that the Sangguniang Bayan Members enact an Ordinance for the creation of AWS, and to be more circumspect in reviewing the proposed Annual Budget submitted by the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, being the LCE, should act with urgency in the mandatory closure of the existing open dumpsite as he may be held liable for violating the solid waste management law.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should regularly appoint a DRRM Officer as required by law.
We recommend that the procurement of regular and ordinary supplies and equipment be made in bulk as required by COA rules and regulations.

We recommend that the BAC should ensure that the Abstract of Canvass or Price Quotation be attached on procurements using the alternative mode of shopping or negotiated procurement as an evidence that they have evaluated the submitted canvass price or price quotation.  Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant should be vigilant in reviewing the supporting documents attached to a claim before processing it.

We recommend that the BAC should prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law.  Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

We recommend that Management discontinue the process of paying for procured goods and services through reimbursement.  We also recommend that Management should adequately plan its procurement and follow strictly the procedures laid down in RA 9184, as well as its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.  Moreover, the Municipal Mayor, being the Head of the Procuring Entity should oversee that all procurement laws are followed.

We recommend to require the concerned officials or its equivalent to strictly observe the provisions of Section 361 of GAAM Volume I on the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels, relative to the use of government vehicles.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, together with its GAD Focal Point System, should oversee and coordinate with the DILG Regional Office in order to facilitate the approval of the Annual GPBs.  Further, we recommend that the Local Sanggunian should first obtain the approved GPB before incorporating it and enacting the Annual Budget of the municipality.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should ensure timely submission of the LGU’s GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team as required by COA regulations.

We recommend that the HRMO, being the Personnel Officer, should refrain from allowing employees to monetize their sick leave credits, and only allow them to monetize vacation leave credits up to the extent allowed and prescribed by CSC Rules and Regulations.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should render a careful evaluation of the requests for special monetization as such is required by CSC rules and regulations.
We recommend that the Management should make a responsive and concrete written policy in granting monetization benefits in such a manner that all of its employees will be benefited.
We recommend that the Management should provide the documents needed to legitimize and justify the grant of CNA incentive to its employees.

We recommend that the Local School Board convene and come up yearly with an Approved Special Education Budget.  We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer should refrain from certifying existence of available appropriations until such time that the Annual School Board budget is approved by the School Board.
